stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 155.2 % and a standard deviation of 15.9 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.1% and 5.2%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 155.2 %. After correcting for the 3.2% bias: ≈ 150.4 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.1%-5.2%) and sampling variability: [140.4, 160.6]. Point estimate: 150.4 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
